script: AddText"Entering the station after docking, you see a large emblem of the Nation emblazoned prominently over the main entrance. Numerous replika units can be seen working in the dockyard, many of them visibly armed. A stern looking replika security officer stops you, and demands that you sign several forms, shoving a rather beaten looking computer pad in your face. After what seems like an eternity, you are finally waved past the checkpoint." textGrayColor

AddText"Arriving at Vineta, you are immediately greeted by a large crowd of people waiting for transport to and from the planet. The planets shattered moon hangs heavy in the morning sky above, a dramatic backdrop to the mundane logistics work going on around you." textGrayColor

AddText"The embarkation area is teeming with a large number of workers and replikas handling cargo containers being loaded onto trams. Some of them sport the famous red sand of Kitezh coating their uniforms. Your cargo manifest is scrutinized with unusual suspicion by the replika security, one of many measures taken to guard against unwanted threats." textGrayColor

AddText"After signing the required forms, make your way out of the spaceport to the main lobby. The logo of the Nation is exceptionally prominent, a massive golden icon towering over the people walking about. Such a patriotic display does little to offset the gloomy conditions outside the building, one made only worse by the endless rows of brutalist apartment complexes. The sooner you can leave this dreary place, the better." textGrayColor

AddText"From orbit, the yellow clouds obscure Heimat's cities. But after descending through them, your shuttle is greeted by an expansive metropolis, no doubt home to the vast majority of the planets population. The well maintained megaport is swarming with activity, and replikas in dress uniforms direct you to the expansive, almost opulent lobby - to sign more paperwork." textGrayColor

AddText"Cargo ships, freight trams and workers are a common sight, even at the passenger areas. It seems that despite being a general port, its mostly used for shipping cargo out. The replika security is more stern and abrasive, and every last one of them is armed. A feeling of fear and suspicion permeates the atmosphere, and most of the workers avoid your gaze." textGrayColor

AddText"The wrecked colonial era terraforming station is a relic from the Domain Era. Destroyed during the war between the Empire and the Nation, only wrecked ships and debris remain. However someone has fixed up the hull breaches and rebuilt the life support. Away from prying eyes of the Replika Protecktors, this is the perfect place to store things."

text: You are not generally well known by the $faction for good or ill. Patrols and officials require the usual level of obedience - and paperwork - from you.

The $faction authorities have a slightly higher opinion of you than other spacers, but but you are still largely unknown. You might be able to purchase surplus military supplies, depending on the mood of who you ask.
Your name is known to some mid-level positions of $theFaction. The mere mention of your name can cause some officials to break out in a sweat or fidget nervously.

You are relatively well-known within $theFaction, whether in person or by reputation. Low level officials will sometimes try to lavish you with gifts so they can win your favor and hopefully help their careers by putting in a good word to their superiors.

You know some of the $faction leaders on a personal level. You will receive trade concessions, high-quality ships, and munitions. Your tales and aid are legendary, and your influence and connections are nigh limitless within the $faction.

$faction officials tend to view you with suspicion. Access to the port authority is granted only grudgingly, and you find yourself the subject of frequent investigations and harassment by Replika security units.
No shoot-on-sight orders have been given but @faction forces are on alert and encouraged to make your life miserable.

Your name is known to officials as a menace to society and top enemy of the people. $Faction commanders have authorization to engage your fleet on sight. However, there may still be a way for you to sneak into their markets to do some black-market trading. You might still recover good standing with $theFaction, but it's going to take some serious work or bribery to do so.

A holo of your face and an IDENT-hash of your last known fleet position are both regular parts of security bulletins issued by Protektor forces. It's fairly certain there's a high-ranking $faction officer sent by top levels of the $theFaction whose primary assignment is to hunt you down. For them, you are enemy number one, a scourge and something to be hated and destroyed.

The bar is crowded with large number of spacers taking a break from their long journey. Of course there are several Replikas but its is hard to discern if they are partaking in the drink or keeping watch over their human counterparts. The bar seems to be selling popular spirits from the sector but thankfully they seem to accept your standard credits as currency. Very few members of the Nation or military members are here, and the ones present all appear to be officials of some kind.

The bar is crowded with large number of spacers taking a break from their long journey. Of course there are several Replikas but its is hard to discern if they are partaking in the drink or keeping watch over their human counterparts. One replika, an EULE unit, seems to be a bartender, seemingly busy cleaning glasses. The bar itself seems to be selling popular spirits from the sector but thankfully they seem to accept your standard credits as currency. Very few members of the Nation or military members are here, and the ones present all appear to be officials of some kind.
Confirming Volksmarine NIS transponder and encryption code. Good to see you $playerName.
Domain-type AIS Code verified. This is $personRank $personName of Eusan Nation. Maintain safe distance from our fleet, and state your intentions, over.
Send word to HQ and request reinforcements! Our forces may be less powerful at the moment but the Nation will prevail!
We are the sword and shield of the Nation! The Volksmarine will not retreat without a fight!
Attention enemies of the Nation. You are outmatched, you are cornered, and you are at our mercy. Surrender now and face justice for your crimes against the Eusan people!

script:
AddText"Hello and thank you for choosing the Eusan Nation. This faction is based off the faction of the same name from Signalis, a horror game created by Barbara Wittmann and Yuri Stern of indie game studio, rose-engine. Many thanks to them for creating a wonderful game that no two people have the same idea of what the story is."

AddText"The Eusan Nation is a totalitarian nation which, in the setting of Starsector, have won their decades long struggle against the Eusan Empire. Now masters of their own star system, they must now contend with the aftermath of the war and the wider Persean Sector. They have been lucky so far with the major powers being rather apathetic of the struggles of the Eusan people. Now with the system finally under total control of the Nation, the stars are theirs for the taking."

AddText"The Nation is primarily a low-tech faction with lots of missile and ballistic weaponry strapped to rather primitive looking starships. What they lack in technology, they make up for it in soft advantages. Their fleet is rather heavy in frigates, destroyers, and cruisers."

AddText"As this mod is under a work in progress, new features will come online as things progress. I hope you enjoy this mod and thank you for choosing this faction to play."

RepGTE "eusan_nation" FAVORABLE

$player.metOfficerYeong == true
$global.officeryeong_neuralrepositorystart == false
eusan_nation_personRelationChecker FAVORABLE

text: She swiftly scans her computer screen. "There has been some departmental infighting recently, and we may have a use for an external mercenary like yourself."

"The name is Ariane Yeong" she says, sliding a rather thin looking folder to you. The cover is littered with redactions and white-out, making a mess of the important text.

The inside is more accommodating to read, and contains several photos of a large building from various angles, or at least what's left of it; charred and twisted metal remains curve out of its split open husk at awkward angles.

"What you're looking at is the Central Neural Archive on Vineta"

"Or at least what remains of it," she continues. "During the war with the Empire, the building was hit in a nuclear bombing run. Much of the important data was destroyed, but not all."

She flips a page in the documentation to an infographic of sorts, parts hastily crossed out and circled.

"AEON is reluctant to hand it over, so an agent was sent to acquire it. We need someone to retrieve the data from him."
options: officeryeongNeuralRepBriefing2:"And how would I go about that?"

text: "Send him a coded message upon arriving at Vineta. Once you set up initial communications, meet with him directly and retrieve the data from him. Fly back to Heimat." She swipes at a strange Tri-Pad adjacent bootleg, sending your fleet the codes and other information.

"You get paid the standard rate according to regulations." Ariane dryly responds.

She hands you an envelope before you leave. "A paper copy of the code phrase. All the less sensitive information has been uploaded to your fleet."

"Alright then. Come back to me when you are ready"
or
"Ok sure. Let me know if you want to reconsider."
"Good. Now the fun begins. For accepting this mission you hereby agree to finish the mission and prioritize its completion for the good of the Nation. Do not come back if you have not finished it."

script:
SetTextHighlights "Recovery Area 5. North Supply depot. Section 11."
ShowPersonVisual
FireAll PopulateAgentOptions
text:
You take a shuttle down to the planet. Flipping through the channels, your comms officer locates the frequency and begins to broadcast the coded signal at the specified time. 

After a few seconds, the line crackles with a response. The comms officer quickly decodes the message and hands you the result.

"Recovery Area 5. North Supply depot. Section 11."

The transmission leads you to an area by a large crater, no doubt the remains of the neural repository. The facility was once used to store and manufacture the neural patterns used by replikas. Now, it is little more than a scar in the ground, surrounded by temporary buildings set up in the vicinity of the impressive scene of destruction.

As you arrive at the meeting spot, you come across a man busy sorting supplies and miscellaneous equipment.

script: SetTextHighlights "data" "I am not sure how I can help, but I will try"
text:
I got a package to deliver, but the address is in Carcosa.

"I am not sure how I can help, but I will try." the man replies. He sizes you up before deciding you are worth trusting.

"You three characters here for the data, right?" the man asks rhetorically. He rips open a spot on his jacket and slides a disk out of it. "You aren't officially affiliated with us, so don't worry about being courteous with AEON. Don't expect them to be courteous to you either, though. You should leave."

Taking the box of data disks from the agent was easy enough. Although no problems were encountered, you cant shake a sense of unease in the back of your mind. Why would the Nation go through this trouble to get this data? And why would AEON put up so many obstacles?

You quash the thought. If its that important, you'll know soon enough. Your shuttle gently lofts into the skies after you board it, the shimmering seas of Vineta fading into the distance.
Your journey back to your flagship is uneventful, the disk secured in your hand.

options: officeryeongNeuralRepFinal:Update Ariane on the status of your mission.
officeryeongNeuralRepCancel:"I have not finished the mission yet."

text:
"Oh I see..." 

You see a small look of disappointment on her face. You probably should have finished the mission prior to asking her.

"Well then... go and finish it. I will be waiting."

options: officeryeongNeuralRepConfirm:"I have what you asked for."

script:
$global.eusan_nation_neuralrepo_completed = true
Call $global.eusan_nation_neuralrepo_ref updateStage
AddStoryPoints 1
FireAll PopulateOptions
text:
You inform her that you were successful in getting the information from the agent. She seems rather pleased with your success.

"Excellent work. Thank you for helping me out." She seems relieved to have the disk in hand, suppressing a smile as she puts it into a bulky device on her desk. She motions for you to leave.

"Your payment should be going through soon."

conditions:
RepGTE "eusan_nation" WELCOMING
$id == "eusan_nation_officer_yeong" score: 100
$player.metOfficerYeong == true
$global.eusan_nation_neuralrepo_completed == true
$global.officeryeong_penroseRecovery1_start == false
eusan_nation_personRelationChecker WELCOMING
options: officeryeongPenroseRecovery1Start:"A new job came from my superiors about search and recovery..."

text:
You sit down in the stiff chair in her cramped office, and she retrieves a pack of cigarettes, lighting one up for herself. She takes a deep puff, before explaining the mission.

"My superiors were pleased you got the data from our agent in Vineta. Whatever was going on with AEON there appears to have been resolved. This new job is a bit more in depth, not just running intelligence errands."

Ariane straightens up and hands you a folder. As you open the folder, the first page is already filled with redactions. Clearly someone was paranoid about secrets getting out.

"Standard procedure. Im sure you know, captain. Much of this information is classified if not to my eyes then yours." Ariane says. She takes another draw from her cig and blows the smoke towards the celling.

"I assume you know about the Penrose Program. If not, it's the Nation's deep space exploration program. Two crew, one human, one replika. Shoved into a deep space scout ship and shot into the expanse."

"And a few weeks ago, the Deep Space Radio Array on Rotfront detected this transmission." Ariane says, pressing a button on a recording device on her desk. Garbled radio noise resolves into a series of beeps, some long, some short. Morse code.

"Im sure you know what these beeps are," she asks, stopping the recording.

Its not Domain standard, but its recognizable in its purpose. A distress signal.

"The transmission is from the PENROSE-419. It was launched over two decades ago. The Flight Officer's name was Cameron Yang. The replika was a standard LSTR unit, LSTR-419. The last time they replied to our hails was 12 years ago. There were a few attempts to reestablish contact, but given 419's mission was essentially over, the Nation did not bother outside the standard hails."

"Given how much time has passed, this is a salvage mission. The mission recorder is to be recovered. The crew, if found, be given last rites as befitting of..." Ariane double checks what she is reading, squinting at the paper in her hands.

"Heroes of the Nation" she states flatly.

"Alright. Here is the important part. The transmission was traced to this part of the galaxy. Our analysts have determined that the Penrose is located $eusan_nation_penroseRecovery1_distanceLy light years away in the $eusan_nation_penroseRecovery1_systemName system and the signal seems to be originating from the planet $eusan_nation_penroseRecovery1_planetName."

"Go there, find the black box mission recorder, and lay the crew to rest."

"Shame. If you want to reconsider, let me know"
OR
"Take your time. Come back later if you want to proceed."
OR
"Ok sure. If you wish to think it through, go ahead. I'll be waiting here once you get yourself together."
"Thank you. I will skip the usual procedures and speech for taking on a mission in the name of the Nation. You know what to do. "
Following the information given to you by Officer Yeong, you arrive at the designated planet. Your comms officer triangulates the rough location of the signal, while a survey team is prepared.
options:
officeryeongPenroseRecovery1Option1:Enter Orbit around the planet
defaultLeave:Leave

text:
As you enter orbit above the planet, your communications officer finally pins the signals location. 

You head over to the communication's officer and begin to examine the data. The transmission is broadcasting on a unique frequency, well outside standard domain protocol. It is significantly clearer and less degraded this close to the source, but still garbled.

Your musing over the data is interrupted by your ops officer reminding you the salvage team is ready.
options: officeryeongPenroseRecovery1Option2:Deploy the recovery teams to the surface

script:
SetTextHighlights "PENROSE-419"
$global.eusan_nation_penroseRecovery1_returnBlackbox = true
$global.eusan_nation_penroseRecovery1_recoveredData = true
Call $global.eusan_nation_penroseRecovery1_ref updateStage
eusan_nation_penroseRecovery1_rulecmd resumeMusic_penroseRecovery1
text:
The shuttle sets down near the wreckage location, and you watch from the body cameras as your salvage team disembarks, fanning out around the hulk.

There was obviously no attempt made to avoid the crash in the ships final moments. Its wreckage is mostly unrecognizable, consisting of a few lucky and durable components scattered hundreds of meters around the impact point. nothing resembling a ship remains.

Miraculously, the blackbox has survived, and with it a digital copy of the logbook. Your team wastes no time recovering them.
options: officeryeongPenroseRecovery1Option3:Order your team to return to the fleet.

text: The team leaves the obliterated survey ship and returns to orbit on the shuttle. A cargo manifest is presented to you well before they dock; only the logbook and blackbox were recovered, but there were other finds left behind as well. Notably, the remains of a replika.

options: officeryeongPenroseRecovery1Final:Update Ariane on the status of your mission.
officeryeongPenroseRecovery1Cancel:Tell her that you have yet to complete the mission.

text:
"So you decided to come and waste my time?"

You can see on her face that she is annoyed by your admission.

"Why are you here? Why are you telling me this."

options: officeryeongPenroseRecovery1ConfirmOption:Tell her that you recovered the mission data recorder.

text:
You hand over the recorder and logs. She hooks them up to a specialized device, which whirrs softly as it loads the ancient files.

You manage to piece together something of a story from the logs, of a proud Vinetan man. He was elated to be accepted onto the Penrose program, and the majority of the logs throughout the first phase of the mission are mundane. The second phase, however, sees conditions aboard deteriorate rapidly, the situation evolving into an unmitigated disaster by the third phase as the ships food supplies are spoiled by a reactor malfunction.

The man describes having found a new source of food which should keep him alive just a little longer. There is no mention of his Replika after that log.

Ariane seems unfazed by the harrowing account of his journey.

"This isn't unusual. At least not in the Penrose Program." 

She sits back on her chair and hands you a mission report from another Penrose mission, PENROSE-211. 

"An earlier mission. Most earlier missions are classified, but I managed to get my hands on this one. This flight officer appears to have made a similar decision. After Phase 3, most Penrose ships will eventually run out of consumables and supplies to keep the ship operating. Reports of desperate behavior among Penrose flight officers are extremely common"

She flips through the pages describing, in apathetic detail, this missions fate.

"You either find a planet, or you fail. PENROSE-419 failed."

script:
SetTextHighlights "PENROSE-419"
$global.eusan_nation_penroseRecovery1_completed = true
Call $global.eusan_nation_penroseRecovery1_ref updateStage
AddStoryPoints 1
FireAll PopulateOptions
text:
She leans in across the desk, talking in a hushed tone.

"I expect the Nation to bury the fate of PENROSE-419, as they have every other mission like it. You may be wondering why the Nation even bothers recovering the remains."

"I don't know, to be honest." She looks around the room, thinking. "Maybe the survey data. Maybe they just want to tie up any loose ends."

She leans back, speaking in a normal tone again.
"As promised, your payment. In Domain standard credits, directly to your personal account. I hope you will continue working with us, and, be quiet about what you found."

conditions:
RepGTE "eusan_nation" FRIENDLY
$id == "eusan_nation_officer_yeong" score: 100
$player.metOfficerYeong == true
$global.eusan_nation_penroseRecovery1_completed == true
$global.officeryeong_penroseRecovery2_start == false
eusan_nation_personRelationChecker FRIENDLY
options: officeryeongPenroseRecovery2Start:"I have another recovery mission for you."

text:
You take a sit at the desk. You've become used to this now. 

"Have a look at this" Ariane says. She hands you a folder, presumably detailing the mission at hand. Like the last one, it's filled with redactions.

"As before, my superiors have decided that you are the best person for the job. Another Penrose recovery, same as before."

"The recovery site is located in this star system, several light years away." She flips to a page with a clean starmap, the target system highlighted.

"However, there is a slight issue. There seems to be a fleet of some kind in the system, a Tri-Tachyon patrol if scavengers are to be believed. Why they're there is anyone's guess. All we know is they are patrolling the location, nothing on the forces size or composition. It could be a Wolf or three Paragons."
options: officeryeongPenroseRecovery2_briefing1:"Hostile fleet, no information on size or composition. Got it."

text:
"As for your mission target:PENROSE-388. This Penrose was launched over two decades ago. I want to make it clear to you:your orders are to destroy the fleet near it. You need to do this before you recover PENROSE-388."

"PENROSE-388 dropped out of contact eighteen years ago. Commanded by a young pilot named Mary Cheng. Well connected. Has ties to a powerful political faction here on Heimat. She's important, and, my superiors have prioritized the recovery of PENROSE-388 above all other similar operations."

"The details on the mission are inside the briefing packet but I will, for brevity's sake, tell you exactly where to go. Signal analysis indicates that PENROSE-388 is located $eusan_nation_penroseRecovery2_distanceLy light years away in the $eusan_nation_penroseRecovery2_systemName system and the craft has crashed on the planet $eusan_nation_penroseRecovery2_planetName"

"Got that? Good. You can look over the details later. Its all here in the file" Ariane says. She opens her drawer and grabs a bright red packet of cigarettes. A familiar, smokey smell begins to permeate the room. She takes a long drag and lets out a puff. 

"That is all. Burn bright." She says the phrase more naturally this time, as if she had practiced it.
options: officeryeongPenroseRecovery2_briefing3:Time for me to go to work then...

text:
"Shame. If you want to reconsider, let me know."
OR
"Take your time. Come back later if you want to proceed."
OR
"Oke. If you wish to think it through, that is fine with me. Come back when you are ready."
"Good luck." She pauses, thinking of something to add. "Burn bright" she says, enunciating the foreign phrase awkwardly.

the Tri-Tachyon fleet commander looks rather disappointed, the extreme disdain for your presence evident on $HeOrShe's face, but only for a moment, years of PR training quickly taking over.

"This is Commander $personName. You are interfering in an official corporate matter, please leave our operational volume."

Following the information given to you by Officer Yeong, you arrive at the designated planet. Your communications officer performs the familiar triangulation protocol, radio noise slowly resolving into the familiar SOS beeps.
options:
officeryeongPenroseRecovery2Option1:Enter Orbit around the planet
defaultLeave:Leave

text:
Entering orbit around the planet, a previously hidden, faint signal appears on scopes.

You manage to locate Penrose-388, and perform a quick high-power sweep of the area. No anomalies appear; Tri-Tachyon either packed up what they were doing here, or are hiding in preparation for an ambush.

The ship itself is surprisingly intact, the crash more similar to an emergency landing than an uncontrolled impact.
options: officeryeongPenroseRecovery2Option2:Deploy the recovery teams to the surface

text:
Your team of scavengers, escorted by several marines, touches down a good distance from the site, the shuttle having followed a standard AA-avoidance flight path. You watch the tac display intently, the team vigilant as they approach the wreckage. Thankfully, they don't encounter anything during the approach.

Your team fans out around the Penrose, cutting into it from several angles and storming the interior. No Tri-Tachyon forces were holed up inside, and the area is confirmed safe.

While the team scours the wreckage for anything important, you order your comms officer to work on getting the signal more coherent. You are then alerted to a new development on the planet.

You watch through the camera feed of one of your marines as they peer into the ships cryo-chamber: the officer is alive. After an approaching technician politely declining the marine's offer to "just rip it open," they begin the thawing procedure.
options: officeryeongPenroseRecovery2Option4:Order the recovery team to return back to the ship

script:
SetTextHighlights "Mary Cheng"
eusan_nation_penroseRecovery2_rulecmd resumeMusic_penroseRecovery2
Call $global.eusan_nation_penroseRecovery2_ref updateStage
text:
Your team stabilizes her after retrieving her from the cryo-chamber, and she is brought to the shuttle. The feed from your marines is cut off as you watch the small craft dart into space as fast as it can manage, hastily docking with your flagship. You greet Mary Cheng as she stumbles aboard, held up by two medics.

She is largely incoherent, but through tears manages to get out a few sentences. "Thank you so much, I knew they would send someone. The Nation wouldn't abandon me, but it was so long..." She tries to shake your hand before she loses her composure completely, but the medics have already dragged her off for treatment.

You recieve a report on her a few hours later: she is in bad shape, but she will make it. She has also requested to join your fleet, to which you give a noncommittal answer.

options: officeryeongPenroseRecovery2Final:Update Ariane on the status of your mission.
officeryeongPenroseRecovery2Cancel:Tell her that you have yet to complete the mission.

text:
"Wasting my time now, is it?"

Your admission to her has visibly annoyed her.

"Please let me know if you've finished your job."

options: officeryeongPenroseRecovery2ConfirmOption:Tell her that you recovered the mission data recorder.

text:
She begins the now familiar routine of decoding the data from the ship once you hand it over to her. Her eyes widen as a certain set of numbers fly across the screen, and she turns towards you with a question.

"Did you find the pilot?"

You recount that Mary Cheng was alive, safely removed from a functional cryo-pod. "They don't usually last that long," she remarks under her breath, as she watches the footage of your salvage crew opening it up.

She pauses a bit. Apparently the news of a Penrose pilot surviving has her thinking.

"Where is she now?" She asks you.
options: officeryeongPenroseRecovery2Confirm1:"She is onboard my ship. Why?"

text:
You respond to Ariane that Mary is currently aboard your flagship. Alive and well but currently recuperating from the long cryo sleep and catching up on all the events that happened since she left home.

"Don't let her come back down here, then." She responds coolly.

You are somewhat surprised by this, but its not difficult to comprehend her motives after a little bit of thought. The Penrose program, more specifically its victims, don't exist. Heads would roll, starting with hers, if you were to ruin that narrative.

"Sooner or later this will get out" she says, as much to herself as you. "For now keep her as part of your fleet."

script:
$global.eusan_nation_penroseRecovery2_completed = true
Call $global.eusan_nation_penroseRecovery2_ref updateStage
eusan_nation_penroseRecovery2_rulecmd add_officer_penroseRecovery2
AddStoryPoints 1
FireAll PopulateOptions
text:
You get up to leave Ariane's office. Before you step through the doorway, Ariane tells you one more thing. 

"Keep her with your fleet, for now, until the foreseeable future. I have sent your payment for a job well done."

You give her a nod and pull out a communications device. You send off a message to your ship to give Mary the news. A short ping from the ship lets you know she is now aware of the situation and happily agrees with staying on as an officer with your fleet.

options: officeryeongPenroseRecovery512Start:I have a new mission, you were specifically requested.

text:
Ariane's request was rather cryptic. Something important must be going on. Your shuttle makes its usual descent to the surface, but on your walk to her office you are stopped just outside of it. A man in some military uniform, its ranks and insignia foreign to the sector, stops you. He seems to think for a moment before nodding, and waving you inside.

Upon entering, you see why. Standing before you and Ariane, is admiral Falke herself.

Your mind is racing, wondering what she could be doing here. Your thoughts are pierced, one becoming louder, clearer than the others. It is not yours.


Good evening, commander.
options:
officeryeongPenroseRecovery512_briefing0_1:Telepathy?
officeryeongPenroseRecovery512_briefing0_2:Get out of my head.
officeryeongPenroseRecovery512_briefing0_3:"How may I be of service to the Nation?"

text:
A crude term. How have you done so much work for us but never encountered this before. Bioresonance is the proper term, and it is more than simple telepathy.

She turns to Ariane, who is pale as a ghost and frozen still in her chair. Finally, she speaks.

"Ariane, explain to them why they are here."
options:
officeryeongPenroseRecovery512_briefing1:Continue
officeryeongPenroseRecovery512_briefing1_1:Why don't you just beam the mission into my head?

text:
There is no need for such hostilities. You will not be harmed. If that was my intent, you would have been already.

The blood drains from Ariane's face, who is probably also hearing Falke's words. She shoots a look pleading you to drop the stand off.

Falke finally speaks. "Ariane, brief them on the mission."

Your devotion is admirable. We have a task for you, requiring your fleet and skills. Ariane will brief you.

Falke glances at Ariane, waiting for her to say something.

Ariane lays a folder on the desk, the redactions and notes on the documents are considerably more neat and professional than the ones for previous missions.

"While you were away, the Rotfront Array detected a signal coming from deep space. Though it was heavily distorted, it is clearly another Penrose distress signal."

She traces her finger across a star map, circling it around a star system marked in red.

"You are to go to this system and recover the source of the signal."
options: officeryeongPenroseRecovery512_briefing2:"A simple recovery mission, yet you are here..."

text:
Falke is visibly annoyed by the suggestion.

It is not a toy, commander. Do not ask me to treat it like one again.

Ariane glares at you with a mixture of horror and surprise, having apparently heard Falke's response. She hurriedly reaches for a folder, presumably the one containing the mission details.

"Lets just get on with the briefing, please."
options:
officeryeongPenroseRecovery512_briefing1:Continue
officeryeongPenroseRecovery512_briefing2A_1:"I've read enough documents, Use your holovid mind powers."

text:
You barely have time to take in Ariane's appalled expression before the onslaught begins.

A detailed list. Of something. Equipment? no, ships. Every Penrose. Built. Launched. Scrapped. All of them, before you.

Scrapped. That single thought is amplified. 512th Penrose, Manufactured, but never launched. Mothballed. This ship never saw the stars.

A piercing wail, wiping your mind of those previous ideas. The noise resolves into a familiar sound. A transmission, not of domain standard. Penrose distress signal.

512

A ship that doesn't exist, on a mission that never began, has issued a distress signal.

You push yourself up off the wall you had unconsciously begun to lean against. Ariane, having dropped the briefing folder, steadies herself as well. She gives you an angry look before beginning to collect the scattered papers with unsteady hands.

"I trust you understand now, Commander?" Falke asks.

Admiral Falke takes a seat, and motions for you to do the same. Ariane holds up a sheet of paper, an inventory of ships and ship parts of the Penrose class, for her to narrate.

"Every Penrose constructed is on this sheet, along with its fate." A not insignificant number of them are noted as 'LOST WITH ALL HANDS'.

"Penrose 512, was never launched. It was partially constructed before being stripped for parts and mothballed. It is currently sitting at a storage depot awaiting final disposal."

Ariane flips to another document. A photograph clearly shows a partially disassembled craft with the hull number "512" in yellowing numbers.

Ariane continues in place of Falke, setting down the papers. "We have interviewed dock workers, checked all the construction and launch relay logs, scoured everything related to the Penrose program, and can find no record of the ship being launched."

"That is what we believe to be the case." Falke stands up, and begins pacing around the room as she says this. "And that is where you come in. Your fleet is well equipped and could investigate the signal, without requiring us to organize an expeditionary force of our own."

The lie is barely disguised. You are expendable, more so than the Nation's own fleets at least. So you will test the waters.

Falke motions to Ariane, perhaps speaking to her telepathically as well. Ariane snaps to attention and tells you the mission info.

"The target is $eusan_nation_penrose512_distanceLy light years away in the $eusan_nation_penrose512_systemName system. and we believe the signal to be from $eusan_nation_penrose512_planetName. Recover everything. The whole ship if you can. And please, record as much of the process as possible."

"Shame. If you want to reconsider, let me know."
OR
"Take your time. Come back later if you want to proceed."
OR
"Ok sure. If you wish to think it through, that is fine with me. Come back when you are ready."
"When you come back, prepare yourself for a long debrief..."

Your fleet approaches the planet, your crew uneasy. Word of the strange nature of the signal appears to have spread further through your crew than it should have, and everyone is on edge. Your sensors pay close attention to every angle of the void, preparing for an ambush, or worse.

Your fleet finishes its approach to the planet. Nothing abnormal about it has been detected thus far, save for the signal itself. 

The comms officer reports the signal is an exact match on what is on file.
options:
officeryeongPenrose512Landing1:Enter Orbit around the planet
defaultLeave:Leave

text:
Having pinpointed the location, you order a team down to the surface, along with a large compliment of marines and whatever heavy arms can be found aboard the fleet. Despite the danger and persistence by your executive officer to dissuade, you decide to head down there as well.

The combat shuttles approach the wreckage from a low altitude, but find nothing out of the ordinary.

As your team approaches the wreck, the crew begin to set up equipment for entry. Due to the cold environment, the work is slow at first but picks up pace as the base camp begins to take form. 

What seems to be an eternity, the crew reports its finished setting up and is beginning the entry to the ship.

The recovery crew finds the airlock and surprisingly they are able to open it without use of external equipment. The recovery team leader reports they are able to enter the ship through the main airlock intact and the interior in satisfactory condition.

The sound of your breath is deafening within the vac suit, interrupted occasionally by chatter between the crew you brought along. Your helmet's beams cut through the dark, stagnant interior, and its visor displays your current location within the ship.

notes: Get picture with cryo code at back of pic

text: The plans indicate this location to be the forward deck of the ship.

script:
SetEnabled officeryeongPenrose512_forwardDecks_start false
SetTooltip officeryeongPenrose512_forwardDecks_start "You need to find a keycard to enter the forward decks and the cockpit..."

script:
SetEnabled officeryeongPenrose512_forwardDecks_start false
SetTooltip officeryeongPenrose512_forwardDecks_start "The door's access panel seems to be flickering on and off..."
text: The plans indicate this location to be the forward deck of the ship.

script:
SetEnabled officeryeongPenrose512_forwardDecks_start false
SetTooltip officeryeongPenrose512_forwardDecks_start "The door bolts are active, holding the door shut. You will need to activate the power and look for a keycard to enter the forward decks and the cockpit...."
text: The plans indicate this location to be the forward deck of the ship.
You and your crew enter the ship.

Wrenching the door open with some difficulty, your salvage crew wave you inside the intact flight deck. The cockpit is completely frosted over, but the seal has held and kept the instruments largely intact. They are unresponsive regardless.
options: officeryeongPenrose512_forwardDecks_explore2aPhoto1:Check out the photo stuck to an instrument panel.

text: Whilst trying to make sense of the instrument panels, you spot a damaged photo attached to it.

script:
$player.officeryeongPenrose512_foundphoto = true
SetTextHighlights "self-developing photo" "Perhaps it would be best if you memorize this combination."
SetTooltip officeryeongPenrose512_forwardDecks_explore2_exit "You feel as if this may come in handy. Perhaps it would be best if you memorize this combination."
KaysaarKeypad initCode
text:
You gently remove the photo from the panel, ensuring you don't damage it. It's a sort of self developing photo, displaying a woman and severely damaged. 

Short-haired and silver haired. She seems happy and proud. Somehow you feel like you seen this person before...

You flip the photo around to see if anything is written but only found a series of red squares under the word KRYO. You don't know what it means yet. Perhaps it would be best if you memorize this combination.
You return to the flight deck. The recovery team has already verified that this area is safe and no structural issues have been found. Perhaps you missed something?
You look at the cockpit and the instrument panel to see if you have missed anything. It took some time but whatever you were looking for, it may already have been recovered already.
Your comms officer informs you that the distress signal's location has been more accurately triangulated, and it is likely somewhere in this room.
Peeling back a panel, your salvors locate the distress beacon, defiantly transmitting in spite of the destroyed electronics surrounding it.

You tune to the frequency with your suit's radio, confirming the beacon is still transmitting. After a few unsuccessful attempts to shut it down through more deft means, your salvage crew wrenches it out of its housing with a crowbar and silences it with a forceful strike.
The plans indicate this location to be the forward deck of the ship.
The recovery crew has already swept this area. Nothing of note is left here save for the debris and damaged equipment.
Leaving behind a skeleton crew, you exit the forward deck.
You enter the hallway, your salvage crew having set up a small staging area here.

You enter the mid section of the ship, navigating around busy salvors and their equipment as they strip and inspect the ship. A medic leans against a nearby wall, their fortunately unused equipment piled nearby.

notes: Player finds a diagram of the ship

text: You were provided with detailed schematics of the vessel, further enhanced by your experience in salvaging the previous two. You bring them up on your tri-pad, observing the layout with the physical map your team salvaged from the area.
You finish looking at the map and ship plans.

notes: Player finds a dead replika and a keycard nearby.

text:
You step into the mess hall, though on such a small ship it served a variety of functions. Including, most importantly, a technical diagnostics area. 

Seems like the power is off. Without power, there is nothing you can gleam here.
notes: power is off. systems monitor is offline

text: You survey the mess hall, the power now on. A previously dead screen has lit up, but displays nothing. You are missing some form of access.
notes: power is on. systems monitor is online. keycard is not found (doors are locked on cryo and forward decks

text: The keycard you found has granted you access to the screen, though it is struggling to display much. Familiar with the system by now, a technician manages to get the image to resolve.
The wireframe model of the ship spins slowly as text is printed across the screen.
As your team continues their work, you decide to leave the area.
You return to the hallway where your team has set up a temporary staging area.

options: officeryeongPenrose512_aftDecks_start:Enter the aft decks of the Penrose

text: The aft decks of the Penrose are the most recent area to be accessed by your crew, and equipment is still being set up.
The crew quarters are a mess, littered with miscellaneous items and debris, and several bags of trash. Many appear to have been broken open and sorted through.
notes: pick up keycard to access forward decks

text: After some time searching through the mess, a salvor triumphantly displays a keycard they have found.
Even more messy now that it has been sorted through, the crew quarters offer little of value.
You perform one more cursory sweep of the room, finding nothing.
options: officeryeongPenrose512_aftDecks2a_exit_option:Go back and search the rest of the deck in this area.

text: You finish searching the crew quarters, deciding to go elsewhere.

notes: Player finds a power grid controller

text: Your crew is resistant to you entering the reactor room, and for good reason. Radiation leaks through openings to the room, and the previously idle medic is now passing out iodine pills, preparing your salvors to enter the room.
options: officeryeongPenrose512_aftDecks_explore2b_power1:Order your crew to stabilize the reactor.

text: You watch on your tri-pad as your crew gets to work on the reactor. It's an ancient, crude fission design, but not simpler for it. Multiple crew rotations are made during the process before the radiation is brought under control, and auxiliary power comes online.
The parts of the ship that remain functional flicker on as the power comes online, and your exhausted crew seals the reactor room off.
You check the power readout given to you by the salvage crew, showing everything to be stable.
options: officeryeongPenrose512_aftDecks_explore2b_exit_option:Go back and search the rest of the deck in this area.

text: The reactor is stable and active. There is nothing else left to do here.
You return again to the staging area in the hallway.

SetEnabled officeryeongPenrose512_cryo_start false
SetTooltip officeryeongPenrose512_cryo_start "You will have to wait until the salvage crew unblocks corridor full of debris. Perhaps you should search the rest of the ship first."

SetEnabled officeryeongPenrose512_cryo_start false

SetEnabled officeryeongPenrose512_cryo_start false
SetTooltip officeryeongPenrose512_cryo_start "Most debris have been removed and power has been restored but there is the issue of finding the access key for the door. Try looking for an access keycard to get through the door."

text: The door to the emergency cryo bay is firmly jammed, and has to be cut open. Occasional beeps from the machinery are the only noises in the room.

The cryo bed itself is intact, like the one on your previous Penrose salvage mission. Could the pilot be alive in this one as well?

You locate the keypad on the sleeper, and flip over the photograph from the flight deck. The grids are the same size.

script:
SetTextHighlights "photo"
SetEnabled officeryeongPenrose512_cryo_explore2a false
KaysaarKeypad initUI
text: The keypad next to the cryo chamber is brightly lit. The 9 digit keypad strangely has no numbers on the keys. Only the correct code will open this chamber.
options:
officeryeongPenrose512_cryo_explore2a:Enter the cryo chamber code.
officeryeongPenrose512_cryo_explore2a_hint:Check the photo for the code.
officeryeongPenrose512_cryo_explore2_exit:Return to the staging area.

text: You remember that photo of the silver haired woman you found and pull it out from your pocket. The woman in the photograph is still happily smiling. You flip the photo on its back and check the combination printed.

script:
SetEnabled officeryeongPenrose512_cryo_explore2 false
SetTooltip officeryeongPenrose512_cryo_explore2 "The cryo sleeper is locked by a 9 digit keycode. You will need to find the code to open the cryo sleeper."
SetTextHighlights "code"
text:
A crewmember tried to unlock the cryo chamber but it is locked. A red flashing light next to a combination lock keypad catches your eye. 

The crewmember tries again. The red light flashes again.

The crewmember gives you a defeated look. Unless you wish to breach the seal and risk the life of the pilot inside, you will need the code to the 9 digit pad to unlock.
options:
officeryeongPenrose512_cryo_explore2a:Use the photo to enter the cryo chamber code.
officeryeongPenrose512_cryo_explore2_exit:Return to the staging area.

text:
Your salvage crew is particularly cautious as the cryo chamber opens. The medic kneels beside you over their bag, sorting through a variety of emergency injection pens.

The doors hiss and open, disappointingly, to an empty chamber. Not so much as a smudge can be spotted on the interior as the water drains from it.

script:
eusan_nation_penrose512Quest_rulecmd resumeMusic_512
$global.eusan_nation_penrose512_returnBlackbox = true
$global.eusan_nation_penrose512_recoveredData = true
Call $global.eusan_nation_penrose512_ref updateStage
text:
The crew mutters over comms, confused and unnerved in equal parts. One salvor hesitantly swipes his hand along the interior of the chamber; nothing aside from beads of residual water is to be found.

You stand there in silence. Already the crew is whispering behind you that the ship is haunted.

Your recovery team hasn't found any members of the crew. No pilot, no replika, no evidence the crew existed. Just remnants of one. Now the cryo chamber you find out, is empty. 

With nothing left to see, you signal to your crew that its time to leave.

You look over the salvage summary as the shuttle ascends. Nothing of particular note was recovered, save for the flight recorder data. Lazily flipping through photos of the operation, the red display panel catches your eye again, with the pilot's name on it. 

A. Yeong.

options: officeryeongPenrose512Final:Update Ariane on the status of your mission.
notes: remember to add branching dialogue for telling ariane about her name on the ship once fluffy adds flag

options: officeryeongPenrose512Cancel:Tell her that you have yet to complete the mission.

text:
"Do we have to do this every time you come here and waste my time?"

She shakes her head over your admission. 

"Please finish the job before you come back and waste my time again."

options: officeryeongPenrose512ConfirmOption:Tell her that you recovered the mission data recorder.

text: For better or worse, Admiral Falke is nowhere to be found as you enter Ariane's office. She is busily shuffling through papers.

She sets down the papers in a clump besides her desk, sitting down in the chair.

"Well? What did you find?"

She takes the tri-pad from your hands, and begins intently reading the salvage report. When she is done, you take it back from her and begin showing her the photos. You stop before showing her the red screen.

"Doesn't seem too out of the ordinary for a recovery op, apart from the empty cryo chamber. Was there anything else?"

You retrieve the damaged photograph from your pocket, the one from the flight deck, and hand it to her.

"Damaged. Photo is a consumer brand common on Rotfront. I used to develop these at a photo store."

"I wonder who this woman is..." Ariane says. She flips the photo to show you the woman. 

A cheerful, smiling, brown haired woman in a Volksarmee uniform can be seen on the damaged photograph. Not the white haired girl on it originally.
"Is there something wrong, $playerName?" she asks, seeing your confusion. She gives the picture another look, then puts the photo into a folder filled with papers.

script:
$global.eusan_nation_penrose512_completed = true
Call $global.eusan_nation_penrose512_ref updateStage
AddStoryPoints 1
FireAll PopulateOptions
text:
"I see." Ariane says. she takes a floppy disk you prepared with the salvage data and stuffs it into the computer.

"This was the last job for you on the docket for now, thanks for your help." She messes with something on the computer before turning back towards you.

"Falke should be here later to look this over. Remember: not a word about this mission leaves your fleet. Dismissed."

options: officeryeongChatPenroseProgramOption:Tell me about the Penrose Program...

text:
"The Penrose Program? If you want to hear about that, it's going to be a long story but let me get down to the public facing summary: the Penrose is the Nation's deep space exploration program. That's it."

"Given that you have high level access I guess I can tell you about the Penrose Program in detail..."

"The Penrose program in reality is an attempt to show the rest of the galaxy the power of the Eusan Nation. Have you seen those posters plastered on the metro lines' streets about the Penrose? Its all about telling us, mainly the people of the Nation, how powerful we are."

"We sent out a lot of these craft into the deep void to find planets we can colonize, mine or just expand the Nation. We pair human pilots with a Replika. In this case the LSTR Replikas. The LSTR types are meant for deep space assignments and repair work. The Volksmarine uses a lot of these replikas on their ships. AEON? Depends on the facility."

"The Penrose program has been operating for a long time. I don't have the details available but rumors say that we launched dozens of these ships during the war. How we did that while a major war was happening is beyond me..."

"Anyway that is all I have. Anything else would require a higher security clearance and I don't want to get in trouble by telling you things that aren't cleared with my superiors..."
options: officeryeongChatPenroseProgramOption3:Very well I won't press the matter further...

text:
"Heimat is the capital of our Nation. I assumed you knew that by now..." she says. She adjusted her uniform a bit and continued on...

"Heimat is where the magic started. A bunch of miners got fed up, overthrew the Imperial garrison here. They used the leftover materials to expand their military forces and build a proper army. The other planets were already rebelling against the Empire with mixed results. Kitezh had its rebellion snuffed out relatively quickly. Vineta's fought on for years until the first reinforcements arrived from Heimat."

"Most, if not all, of the Nation's government agencies reside here on this planet. Volksmarine, Volksarmee, AEON, every branch of government... "

"You know, initially the government was supposed to relocate to Vineta after the war but they did a survey of the planet and found out that the planet was just too damaged to consider relocating. Also Heimat was already functioning as the capital of the Nation so it made little sense to relocate to Vineta. Aside from that, I guess thats the basics of Heimat."

"Heimat is the home of the Nation's government. That is pretty much it."

"Rotfront...yeah..."

"Rotfront was my home for a good long time. I spent many years of my childhood with my mother cooped up inside a radio station. I had lots of fond memories there. Read a lot of books, did art and pretty much had much of the station to myself. Homeschooled for most of my childhood too."

Her voice trailed off and you can tell there were some unpleasant memories resurfacing.  

"Then one day my mother told me I was to live with my aunt Kamilla. So I moved from the radio station to the main city at Blockwart C..."

"Went to high school there. Never liked it. Bullies everywhere." She says, motioning to her hair and eyes as she went on. "Made fun of my hair, my eyes, my family. Made friends of course, but that is a story for another time."

"Military service done there at Rotfront, trained and operated as a radio operator. Spent 2 years doing that. Never knew what happened to my friends after high school. Tried getting hold of them but no response to my letters or requests..."

"Anyway, enough chit chat about Rotfront. The less we talk about it, the better..."

"Was born on Leng. I do not have many memories of that place because my mother moved us to Rotfront due to her position as a radio officer."

"As far as I can tell, Leng is the outermost planet of the star system. The Nation's furthest outpost. A shit hole from what I gathered. Dodged a bullet right there too. They wanted to send me to Leng after my mandatory military service. I applied for a different military service and got accepted...barely from what I was told."

"Leng is not a nice place from what I heard. Unlike Buyan or Rotfront, Leng is literally an airless rock with tunnels for human habitation. You got the orbital and the elevator. Then you have the various rail and tram lines to whatever areas you need to go."

"Also being Leng, the place apparently has a reputation of dread. The rumors of that place housing AEON worksites. All hush-hush rumors of course, but the rumors are: that it's not a nice place to live, work, or have a family."

"That is all I have about Leng for my clearance level."

options: officeryeongChatSmokingOption:So I noticed you smoke a lot...

text: "Smoking?" she queries. "What about it?"
options: officeryeongChatSmoking1:"You know thats bad for you. It could kill you."

text:
She seems both annoyed and amused by your comment. As if you have missed some sort of irony in you, a spacer, chastising someone for undertaking a dangerous activity.

"Yes I smoke. So what..."

"Sure. What do you want to discuss?"
OR
"I can spare some time to talk for a bit. What do you want to talk about?"
OR
"Ask away."
OR
"What do you want to know?"

"Mmm hmm... let me know if you want to know more information..."
OR
"Talk to you later then..."
OR
"Remember, whatever we speak here, should probably not be said anywhere else. Just a precaution."
OR
"See you next time..."

options: officeryeongGiftLobsterOffer:Offer Officer Yeong a lobster from Volturn

script:
AddRemoveCommodity lobster -1 true
AdjustRepActivePerson WELCOMING 5
$player.officeryeonggiftgive = true 7
FireAll PopulateOptions
text:
"Thanks for the gift of lobster. Now I don't have to eat the ration packs they give us." $personLastName says. "Do you know how many times I have eaten the same ration pack this last week?"

$personLastName takes the gift from you. She seems slightly pleased with the fact she isn't going to eat the same dish this evening. 
OR
"Oh? A lobster? The last time I had lobster was when i was a child. My mother managed to get lobster from Vineta but even then it was a bit old."

You hand her the lobster. She gives you a small nod in appreciation.
OR
"Oh my, lobster? How did you get this? Did you swipe it from a shipment or bought it from a market? In fact, don't tell me. I don't want to know..."

She takes the lobster off your hands. You swore you can see a faint smile as she takes the gift.

options: officeryeongGiftLobsterAsk:Ask Officer Yeong how she feels about Volturn Lobsters

text:
"Lobsters? I do not have time to get anything fancy for food, not that I can get them anyway. I've only eaten lobster once and it was for a special occasion. That was years ago."

"Wait... what do you mean blue lobsters?"
OR
"Lobsters... from Volturn? I have heard of them but I've never seen one in person. I assume they have claws and stuff..."

"I bet it costs a lot just to ship them here."
OR
"I am not sure. Volturn is far away and I don't have time to fly around or travel. Even if I had the ability, I would need to pass checkpoint after checkpoint just to leave this planet. Not worth the time and effort just to sample a dish."

"Maybe one day..."
OR
"Honestly, the rumor is that lobsters are shipped here as food for the upper echelons of the Nation's government here. I never seen any type of seafood here, Volturn or from Vineta."

"Why you ask? Are you trying to get me one?"

options: officeryeongGiftLobsterPlushOffer:Offer Officer Yeong a soft plush lobster toy

script:
AddRemoveCommodity sfcgift6 -1 true
AdjustRepActivePerson WELCOMING 1
$player.officeryeonggiftgive = true 14
FireAll PopulateOptions
text:
"Thanks for the gift." $personLastName says.  "It'd be hard to keep this gift because contraband isn't allowed and I am pretty sure this is contraband."

She looks happy but as she moves away you can see her edging towards the trash bin...
OR
"What a pitiful looking mascot. I love it." As $personLastName takes the plush lobster from you. "Thanks for the gift but please don't give me things like this in the future. I can get in trouble..."

She gives the plush toy a small pat and disappears into her office. You hear the clunk of a trash bin lid.
OR
"The laws of the Nation are pretty strict. I can't recieve gifts or they will be considered bribes." $personLastName says. "Fortunately, I have the means of keeping gifts... or sanitizing them. Thanks anyway."

She gives you a faint nod and she walks away with the plush. You swore you could have heard the disposal chute turn on...

options: officeryeongGiftLobsterPlushAsk:Ask Officer Yeong how she feels about a stuffed toy lobster

text:
"A plush lobster? I honestly don't know."
OR
"A toy? Can I even have something like that?..."
OR
"I am a bit old to have toys..."

"Maybe..."

options: officeryeongGiftChocoLavaOffer:Offer Officer Yeong a chocolate lava cake

script:
AddRemoveCommodity uaf_bakery_choco_lava -1 true
AdjustRepActivePerson WELCOMING 5
$player.officeryeonggiftgive = true 5
FireAll PopulateOptions
text:
"Huh? Interesting. I have never seen this type of dessert before." $personLastName says. "This is actually pretty good. Where does it come from?"

She pokes the cake a bit...chocolate filling begins to ooze out.

"In fact, don't tell me. It's better if I don't know." 
OR
"Oh my, what is this thing? A cake? And it's filled with a sweet gooey filling? How decadent... Nothing like this comes to my desk." $personLastName says. You can tell she is exceptionally fascinated by the cake you gave her.

She gives the cake a small nibble. Chocolate filing oozes out and she gives a small smile.

"Thanks... This means a lot to me."
OR
"You really should not be giving me gifts. It is considered illegal and against the laws... I could get in trouble... Is that cake? With a sweet filling?" $personLastName says. She moves her hand to poke the filling that oozed out. She gives it a small lick and her eyes brighten.

"This is really good. Thanks for the gift!"

options: officeryeongGiftChocoLavaAsk:Ask Officer Yeong about chocolate lava cakes

text: "test"

"Wait you were going to give me something?"
OR
"Perhaps it'd be best if you did not."
OR
"Whatever..."

ShowPersonVisual
$menuState = fleetConvMain 0

AddText "Your request for communications is granted and the screen begins to display a live video of a confidant looking woman with a faint smile on her lips."
AddTextSmall "Admiral Falke of the Eusan Nation. Commanding the Ever Victorious Fleet of the Heimat Defense Flotilla. She is unusual as she is not human but a Replika, a type of cybernetic organism built to serve the Nation. Commanding an entire fleet speaks volumes on the Nation's trust in her ability to lead. She is the defender of the Flame of Revolution. She is the torchbearer of the Nation. She is Falke." highlight
AddText ""Why hello there, comrade. How can I help you? Just remember, were both officers of the Nation so its only natural to help one another.""

She is the defender of the Flame of Revolution. She is the torchbearer of the Nation. She is Falke." highlight
AddText "Why hello there fellow traveller. You seem lost. Its only natural you come to me to ask for my help. So...how can I help you?"

text:
"Hello there, comrade. How are you today?"
OR
"Working hard for the betterment of the Nation? I am."
OR
"Hello hello, comrade. Another wonderful day in the service of the Nation."
OR
"Many have tried to cross me, none survived. Comrade, it is up to us to defeat those who drag us down."
OR
"We carry the torch of the Revolution in our hands, comrade."
OR
"Greetings and salutations, comrade. May the light of the Nation guide you on your travels."
OR
"Comrade! It's good to see you. You must have so many stories to tell us."
OR
"Back again, Comrade? My golden radiance is irresistible, yes, I know."
OR
"I see you, Comrade. Welcome."

"Ah hello there, traveller. Have you come to witness my radiance?"
OR
"Hello there, traveller. You are a long way from home."
OR
"What brings you to our star system, dear traveller?"
OR
"Greetings and salutations, dear traveller. May the stars shine bright on your travels."
OR
"Hello traveller. I am fortunate to have met you. One day, I hope you join our Nation in glorious victory."
OR
"If the Nation made any mistakes, perhaps one of them is not having you be a friend earlier. "
OR
"One day we will have victory. The Nation will certainly be glad to have you as a loyal comrade for the day we achieve it."

"Greetings comrade. Make sure you behave."
OR
"Comrade, my time is limited, my patience is even more so."
OR
"Have you come to bask in the glory of the Nation? Or have you just decided to waste my time?"
OR
"Perhaps you should reflect on how to improve yourself. There is still time."
OR
"The stars are very unforgiving without friends and allies. Its not recommended to wander without a guardian."
OR
"One day victory will be ours. The Nation will ply the stars flying our banner. Either you stand with us together in victory or you will be left behind."
OR
"I show mercy to those who submit. If you decide to behave I might let you live."
OR
"Oh my. Have you come to talk to me? I hope you have something interesting to say if you are this desperate to bother me."
OR
"The Empire was defeated long ago but our vigilance will never cease. I hope you understand that. Its only natural to be suspicious of misbehavior."

"Greetings traveller. You have entered the sovereign territory of the Nation. Make sure you behave."
OR
"Traveller, my time is limited, my patience is even more so. My fleet stands ready. Understand your place."
OR
"Have you come to bask in the glory of the Nation? Or have you just decided to waste my time?"
OR
"Perhaps you should reflect on how to improve yourself. There is still time."
OR
"The stars are very unforgiving without friends and allies. Its not recommended to wander without a guardian."
OR
"One day victory will be ours. The Nation will ply the stars flying our banner. Either you stand with us together in victory or you will be left behind."
OR
"I show mercy to those who submit. If you decide to behave I might let you live, traveller."
OR
"Oh my. Have you come to talk to me? I hope you have something interesting to say if you are this desperate to bother me."
OR
"The Empire was defeated long ago but our vigilance will never cease. I hope you understand that. Its only natural to be suspicious of travellers, including you."

"Many have tried to cross me. None have survived. Come now, lets dance."
OR
"You stand before a literal GOD. Either surrender or be smote by the power of the Nation's finest."
OR
"A little fly has wandered into my trap. It is time for you to understand how a spider eat's its prey."
OR
"The Nation demands justice and I am its instrument."
OR
"The last chapter of your life is being written by my hands. Come and lets see how it ends."
OR
""Perhaps its time to end this sad chapter of your life. If you wanted mercy, you should have fought harder."

She gives you a soft smile and disarming look. 

"Oh so you wish to know more about the Volksmarine, hmm?" Admiral Falke says. 

"The Volksmarine, as you may know, is the Nation's primary military arm dedicated to defending against our enemies that ply the stars."

"For many decades we fought against our hated foes, the Empire, for control of this star system. Our humble beginnings began on Heimat using repurposed cargo freighters and civilian shuttle ferries armed with whatever weapons we can put together."

"We could never match the Empire ship to ship. They had better ships, guns, trained crews, etc. They outclassed us at everything."

"When we finally reached Buyan, our fleet was a far cry from the improvised cargo ships with guns strapped to the hull. We had purpose built capital ships, and an officer corps blooded from the most vicious engagements against the Empire. Our final battle was bloody and hard fought but the victory was decisive."

"After the war, we expanded our fleet. We do not have the luxury of letting our guard down. The Volksmarine contends with threats from a variety of vectors. We have ongoing issues with the Hegemony, the Persean League, and Tri-Tachyon. Pirates and religious extremists shouting about the evils of technology tests our patience every day."

"Just remember, the Volksmarine is to defend the Nation and its interests." Falke says. "Where the Nation goes, the Volksmarine is there. We fight for the revolution. We fight to keep it."

She gives you a small smile. 

"I believe that is all for now. It is time for me to go."

options: admiralFalkeGrandFuelFleetOption: Tell me about the Grand Fuel Fleet.

text:
"The Grand Fuel Fleet?" Admiral Falke responds. She puts a finger on her lips as if pondering her next words but it is clear she knows who you met.

"Ah yes. Them. If there was any military formation that is full of pomp and predatory instinct it would be the Grand Fuel Fleet of the Sindrian Fuel Company. Their commanding officer is rather vicious opponent should you ever try to oppose her."

"Then yet again, there is some truth to her reputation. For most people, she is the flame that draws the unfortunate moths to their doom."

"The Grand Fuel Fleet itself is also not to be taken lightly. They consist of the most powerful warships assigned to the Sindrian Fuel Comany fleet. The Sindrians are not idle in their pursuit of fuel profits. They have spent years producing weapons that can make other powers like the Hegemony and Leauge pause."
"Should you attempt unwise dealings with them, do try not to get burned. There is only so much you can do to stop such a person."
"In any case, the Grand Fuel Fleet is something to behold. Do tread lightly around them."

"Hello there. What do you wish to speak about?"
OR
"Ah, more conversation?"
OR
"Do ask your questions. I love a good conversation."
OR
"Talk? I can certainly can."
OR
"My my, coming back for more. I can definitely accommodate."
OR
"Consulting with me? I can make the time."
OR
"Returning I see."

"You know where to find me."
OR
"You will return. I am sure of it."
OR
"Safe travels."
OR
"Goodbye, then. The Nation bids you well in your travels."
You see a Replika busy cleaning some glasses behind the bar counter. You catch her eye and she gives you a small hand wave to come over...

text:
Marlis reached under the table and pulls out a pack of cigarettes. The packaging was simple yet elegant enough to pass for quality.

"Here you go! Enjoy." she says as she hands you the pack.
options: barMarlis_cigs_intro:Look at the other items for sale.

text:
Marlis reached under the table and pulls five packs of cigarettes. The packaging was simple yet elegant enough to pass for quality.

"Oh my. Stocking up? No worries. Just remember not to smoke all at once." she says as she hands you all five packs.

"See you later!"
OR
"Come back anytime"

The replika is refusing to serve you. Perhaps its time to leave.
OR
The replika gives you a glowering look. You are not welcome by the looks of it.

AddTextSmall "The Great Revolutionary, in her glory. You heard stories of the Nation's leader no doubt, how she succeeded the previous leader, how she steered the Nation rebuilding from a ruinous war. Of course, these stories are propaganda but one can't dismiss the political skill needed for her to rule for over twenty years. For an outsider, the honor of having an audience with the Great Revolutionary is rare and not given lightly. text:
"Hello Comrade, how are you today."
OR
"Heimat welcomes you, comrade."
OR
"The Nation welcomes you, comrade."
OR
"Welcome back, Comrade. How goes your travels?"
OR
"You should tell me more about your travels one day."
OR
"Busy as always but you have my attention."
OR
"Comrade, what brings you back to Heimat?"

"Hello traveller, how are you today."
OR
"Traveller, you should consider joining the Nation. The benefits are...suitable for you."
OR
"Weclome back, traveller."
OR
"I see you have returned, traveller."
OR
"Hmph. Back so soon I see. What can I help you with?"
OR
"Governing the Nation is exhausting so please tell me what you need."
OR
"I knew you were going to call so I took the liberty of making some time. Speak."

"Doing enough to scrape by, I see."
OR
"I was told you were in the system. What do you want."
OR
"Traveller, your standing with the Nation could use some improvement. Take it as an hint."
OR
"This better be good."
OR
"Your commission with the Nation was done out of kindness. Work to keep it."
OR
"Remember, traveller, everytime you move a muscle, I know what action you may take."
OR

"Traveller. Your actions worry me and the rest of the Nation. Perhaps its time to find a way to rectify that."
OR
"You are wasting precious time. Speak."
OR
